summary: Bacteriocidals like bacitracin are effective at preventing and treating bacterial infections by targeting pathogen-infected sites directly; they work topically within a defined area around an injury or wound without broader systemic impact beyond initial application for combating established microbial threats leading us towards identifying 'zones' as their appropriate habitat during active battles against invaders near entry portals into host body systems through physical barriers breached via trauma events resulting from cuts abrasions etc... To summarize further concisely while maintaining core message intact: Topical bactericidal toxins act specifically upon contaminated/infectious regions curbing spread & proliferation thereby confining battlefields precisely delineating boundaries necessary conditions met allowing precise deployment strategies optimizing outcomes minimizing collateral damage elsewhere throughout rest organism physiology functioning normally away direct line-of-fire engagement scenarios involving hostile microorganisms attempting breach natural defenses present along periphery vulnerable spots prone
